name: Unnamed south-road village
region: Northern Lordaeron, south of Millhaven on the road toward Andorhal

sensoryProfile.sight: Morning wagons, damp thatch, bread sellers, coughing villagers, fever pallets in the common house, and eight ordinary grain sacks standing against the granary wall.
sensoryProfile.sound: Market noise cut by coughs, a dog behind the bakehouse, a mule being shouted at, then the wrong hush that gathers around the granary and the closet door.
sensoryProfile.smell: Bread smoke, wet mud, horse dung, common-well iron, fever-sour bedding, and the sweet wrongness of plague under all of it.
sensoryProfile.touch: Cold stone in the infirmary, damp wool, grain dust on wood and gloves, and sticky residue from the broken concentrated agent.
sensoryProfile.atmosphere: The horror here is not emptiness but ordinary life still trying to continue while the plague is already inside food, bodies, and routine.

establishedFacts.fact: The village is still functioning on the surface when Jaina and Sylvanas arrive, making it the first on-page look at the plague before full collapse.
Its sick are being housed in the back room of the common house under Tomas Brek's improvised care.
Bread eaters show the worst cases, while villagers on oats or separate stores are lighter touched.
Eight sacks in the granary are tainted, and the contamination can be carried in concentrated liquid form before it is worked through the grain.
A live watcher, Davan, was stationed inside the granary and carried poison for capture.

eventsHere.event: Jaina confirms the living outbreak clusters in the gut and proves grain ingestion is the carrier.
Sylvanas builds the supplier pattern from ledgers, timings, and witness routine until it locks against Jaina's diagnosis.
The granary exposure reveals concentrated plague agent, a live operative, and the deliberate nature of the network.
Davan dies by carried poison before interrogation, and the surviving party fixes Andorhal as the next target.
